item.title: Discovering Descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
This taôÙlk was preôÙsenôÙted in PyôÙCon CZ 2017. HeôÙre aôÙre the reôÙfeôÙrenôÙces and reôÙsourôÙces for the preôÙsenôÙtaôÙtioôÙn.

The coôÙde usôÙed for the taôÙlk, is avaiôÙlaôÙble at httpôÙs://ôÙgisôÙt.ôÙgiôÙthuôÙb.ôÙcoôÙm/rôÙmaôÙriaôÙnoôÙ/a359ôÙfe6ôÙb0ôÙc650589ôÙdf68ôÙc9619ôÙc9354ôÙf0 and conôÙtains soôÙme exôÙplaôÙnaôÙtions along wiôÙth the examôÙpleôÙs.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 1 minutos de lectura)

item.title: PyCon CZ - Day 1
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
The day started at 9.00, and at first it was tiôÙme of cheôÙck-iôÙn, etc. After thaôÙt, I solôÙved one riôÙddôÙle by kiwi.ôÙcom, and earôÙned a disôÙcount in fliôÙghôÙts, whiôÙch was a niôÙce way to start the conôÙfeôÙrenôÙce.

Then after breakôÙfast and soôÙme neôÙtwoôÙrking goôÙing thôÙrouôÙgh the booths of the sôÙponôÙsorôÙs, it was tiôÙme for the first taôÙlk of the daôÙy: "When bugs biôÙte - whôÙy ôÙneôÙglecôÙting edge caôÙses can kiôÙll".

It was a great taôÙlk, a caôÙse for soôÙftwaôÙre enôÙgiôÙneeôÙring in geôÙneôÙral (iôÙt's noôÙt ôÙPôÙyôÙthoôÙn-sôÙpeôÙciôÙfiôÙc, whiôÙch is what makes the toôÙpic moôÙre inôÙteôÙresôÙtinôÙg). The besôÙt ôÙthing I liked about the taôÙlk was the reôÙmaôÙrks, and the idea of shiôÙfting the ôÙminôÙdôÙset when it coôÙmes to deôÙveôÙloôÙpôÙmenôÙt, so we should do soôÙme "neôÙgaôÙtiôÙve thiôÙnkinôÙg" in the form of what can go wrong wiôÙth this? How can this faiôÙl?, and suôÙch. IôÙt's imôÙporôÙtanôÙt, beôÙcauôÙse this is criôÙtiôÙcal in orôÙder to make roôÙbust soôÙftwaôÙre. MosôÙt ôÙproôÙbleôÙms I haôÙve seen on soôÙftwaôÙre bugs weôÙre reôÙlated to soôÙme opôÙtiôÙmisôÙtic thiôÙnkinôÙg, and even worôÙse, opôÙtiôÙmisôÙtic unit tesôÙtinôÙg, in the way that deôÙveôÙloôÙpers onôÙly tesôÙt ôÙfor haôÙppôÙy-ôÙpaôÙth sceôÙnaôÙrioôÙs, wiôÙthout thiôÙnking all sort of things that can goôÙ wôÙronôÙg.

AfteôÙrwarôÙdôÙs, was the tiôÙme for the taôÙlk about paôÙraôÙllel proôÙceôÙsôÙsing (poor perôÙsoôÙn's ôÙpaôÙraôÙllel proôÙceôÙsôÙsing), whiôÙch was fiôÙne. Then I spent soôÙme tiôÙme taôÙckôÙling soôÙme of ôÙthe chaôÙllenôÙges sponôÙsors had avaiôÙlaôÙble, so I did soôÙme coôÙding and reôÙcap of the eôÙvenôÙts so faôÙr.

Then I lisôÙteôÙned to a taôÙlk about wolôÙfcrôÙypt whiôÙch is a tool foôÙr crôÙypôÙto in PyôÙthoôÙn. The taôÙlk inôÙtroôÙduôÙced soôÙme crôÙypôÙto conôÙcepôÙts (sôÙyôÙmôÙmeôÙtriôÙc crôÙypôÙto, puôÙblic keôÙy, etc.), whiôÙch was good. Most of the quesôÙtions reôÙvolôÙveôÙd aôÙround comôÙpaôÙriôÙson wiôÙth other tools in pyôÙthon (deôÙfault liôÙbraôÙrieôÙs, pyôÙcrôÙypôÙto, eôÙtc.).

Then it was tiôÙme for lunôÙch and do soôÙme moôÙre coôÙdinôÙg, and the next taôÙlk I aôÙttenôÙded to was caôÙlled "should I moôÙck or should I noôÙt?", whiôÙch I liked veôÙrôÙy ôÙmuôÙch and gaôÙve soôÙme food for thouôÙghôÙt.

Then it caôÙme one moôÙre taôÙlk, after whiôÙch it was tiôÙme for miôÙne, so I preôÙsenôÙteôÙd clean coôÙde in PyôÙthon. TheôÙre weôÙre soôÙme inôÙteôÙresôÙting quesôÙtionôÙs, and the enôÙtiôÙre ôÙpreôÙsenôÙtaôÙtion went reôÙlaôÙtiôÙveôÙly quiôÙckôÙlôÙy.

OnôÙce the taôÙlk sesôÙsions weôÙre oveôÙr, theôÙre was one last traôÙck for liôÙghôÙtning taôÙlks, whiôÙch are alwaôÙys suôÙper enôÙterôÙtaiôÙninôÙg.

After the fiôÙnish of the first daôÙy, I attenôÙded the speakeôÙr's dinôÙneôÙr, whiôÙch was a ôÙniôÙce opporôÙtuôÙniôÙty to neôÙtwoôÙrk wiôÙth the coôÙmôÙmuôÙniôÙty, whiôÙch is alwaôÙys greaôÙt.

Looking foôÙrward to soôÙme moôÙre inôÙteôÙresôÙting taôÙlks toôÙmoôÙrroôÙw, and to see the reôÙsulôÙts of the chaôÙllenôÙges.

item.title: The __set_name__ method for descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
DesôÙcripôÙtors geôÙneôÙraôÙlly haôÙve to inôÙteôÙract wiôÙth attriôÙbuôÙtes of the maôÙnaôÙged obôÙjecôÙt, and this is doôÙne by insôÙpecôÙting __ôÙdicôÙt__ on that obôÙject (or caôÙllinôÙg geôÙtaôÙttr/seôÙtaôÙttr, but the proôÙblem is the saôÙme), and finôÙding the key unôÙdeôÙr ôÙthe speôÙciôÙfic naôÙme.

For this reaôÙsoôÙn, the desôÙcripôÙtor wiôÙll haôÙve to know the naôÙme of the key to look ôÙfoôÙr, whiôÙch is reôÙlated to the naôÙme of the attriôÙbuôÙte is maôÙnaôÙginôÙg.

On preôÙvious verôÙsions of PyôÙthon this had to be doôÙne exôÙpliôÙciôÙtlôÙy. If we wanted toôÙ woôÙrk around it, theôÙre weôÙre soôÙme moôÙre adôÙvanôÙced waôÙys to do so. LuôÙckiôÙlôÙy, afteôÙr PEôÙP-487 (aôÙdded in PyôÙthon 3.6), ôÙtheôÙre are soôÙme enhanôÙceôÙmenôÙts reôÙgarôÙding claôÙss creaôÙtioôÙn, whiôÙch alôÙso affecôÙts ôÙdesôÙcripôÙtorôÙs.

LeôÙt's reôÙview the proôÙbleôÙm, the preôÙvious approôÙaches to taôÙckôÙle it, and the moôÙderôÙn way of solôÙving it.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 4 minutos de lectura)

item.title: Descriptors & Decorators
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
DesôÙcripôÙtors are an amaôÙzing tool to haôÙve in our toolôÙboôÙx, as they coôÙme in handôÙy in many opporôÙtuôÙniôÙtieôÙs.

ProôÙbaôÙbly the best thing about desôÙcripôÙtorôÙs, is that they can imôÙproôÙve otheôÙr ôÙsoôÙluôÙtionôÙs. LeôÙt's see how we can wriôÙte beôÙtter deôÙcoôÙraôÙtorôÙs, by using desôÙcripôÙtorôÙs.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 5 minutos de lectura)

item.title: Types of Descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
ReôÙsuôÙming from wheôÙre we leôÙft off, on the preôÙvious posôÙt, on whiôÙch we took A first look at desôÙcripôÙtors, it's tiôÙme to exôÙploôÙre their diôÙffeôÙrent tyôÙpes and how they woôÙrk inôÙterôÙnaôÙllôÙy.

In Python, almost everything is represented with a dictionary. Objects are dictionaries. Classes are objects, hence they also are contained into a dictionary. This is denoted by the __dict__ attribute that objects have.

There are two types of descriptors: data descriptors and non-data ones. If a descriptor implements both [1] __get__() and __set__(), it's called a data descriptor; otherwise is a non-data descriptor.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 7 minutos de lectura)

item.title: A first look at descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
DesôÙcripôÙtors are one of the most poôÙwerôÙful feaôÙtuôÙres of PyôÙthoôÙn. The reaôÙson whôÙy ôÙtheôÙy'ôÙre so poôÙwerôÙful is beôÙcauôÙse they enaôÙble us to conôÙtrol the coôÙre opeôÙraôÙtionôÙs (ôÙgeôÙt, seôÙt, deôÙleôÙte) [1], of an attriôÙbuôÙte in a giôÙven obôÙjecôÙt, so that we can hook a parôÙtiôÙcuôÙlar coôÙde, conôÙtroôÙlled by us, in orôÙder to moôÙdiôÙfy, chanôÙge, or exôÙtend the oôÙriôÙgiôÙnal opeôÙraôÙtioôÙn.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 8 minutos de lectura)

item.title: Upcoming talk at PyCon CZ 2017
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
The proôÙpoôÙsal of my taôÙlk tiôÙtled "DisôÙcoôÙveôÙring DesôÙcripôÙtors", was acôÙcepôÙted foôÙr ôÙthe next ediôÙtion of PyôÙCon CZ, in PraôÙgue, CzeôÙch ôÙReôÙpuôÙbliôÙc. AlôÙthouôÙgh the scheôÙduôÙle is not yet readôÙy, this taôÙlk wiôÙll most likeôÙly beôÙ on JuôÙne 8th.

The first two daôÙys of the conôÙfeôÙrenôÙce (JuôÙne 8th and JuôÙne 9th), wiôÙll be for taôÙlks, whilst the last one (SôÙaturôÙdaôÙy, JuôÙne 10), wiôÙll be for woôÙrksôÙhops (sôÙprinôÙts).

This is anoôÙther inôÙteôÙresôÙting opporôÙtuôÙniôÙty to shaôÙre and learn from the PyôÙthoôÙn ôÙcoôÙmôÙmuôÙniôÙty, as it was last year when I preôÙsenôÙteôÙd at EuôÙroôÙPôÙyôÙthon 2016. On this ocôÙcaôÙsioôÙn, I ôÙplan to post an enôÙtry wiôÙth the evenôÙts, per eaôÙch day of the conôÙfeôÙrenôÙce.

item.title: __wrapped__ in Python decorators
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
This is anoôÙther of the new inôÙteôÙresôÙting things that PyôÙthon 3 haôÙs. TiôÙme ago, it was soôÙmeôÙhow triôÙcky to woôÙrk wiôÙth deôÙcoôÙraôÙted funcôÙtionôÙs, beôÙcauôÙse the deôÙcoôÙraôÙtoôÙr ôÙreôÙplaôÙced the oriôÙgiôÙnal obôÙjecôÙt, and its beôÙhaôÙviour beôÙcaôÙme hard to reaôÙch.

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 2 minutos de lectura)

item.title: Python Barcelona November meetup notes
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Last ThursôÙday NoôÙvemôÙber 17, 2016, theôÙre was a PyôÙthon meeôÙtup in BarôÙceôÙloôÙna [1], wiôÙth a set of inôÙteôÙresôÙting taôÙlks.

The first one was hosôÙted by two reôÙpreôÙsenôÙtaôÙtiôÙves of the goôÙvernôÙment of CaôÙtaôÙlunôÙya, and BarôÙceôÙloôÙna ciôÙty, and they preôÙsenôÙted the teôÙchôÙniôÙcal chaôÙllenôÙges they are ôÙfaôÙcinôÙg, and the new staôÙck proôÙpoôÙsed for onôÙcoôÙming proôÙjecôÙts. In this reôÙgarôÙd, it was inôÙteôÙresôÙting to see how they haôÙve loôÙts of leôÙgaôÙcy appliôÙcaôÙtions wriôÙtten in ôÙJ2EE, wiôÙth JaôÙva fraôÙmewoôÙrks, that are ouôÙtdateôÙd, diôÙffiôÙcult to mainôÙtaiôÙn, and theôÙy ôÙmenôÙtioôÙned the idea of miôÙgraôÙting them to new, moôÙre moôÙdern teôÙchôÙnoôÙloôÙgieôÙs. In this senôÙse, theôÙre are alôÙready proôÙjecôÙts in proôÙgress, and they choôÙse PyôÙthon + DjanôÙgoôÙ ôÙfor the miôÙgraôÙtion and re-imôÙpleôÙmenôÙtaôÙtion of the leôÙgaôÙcy sysôÙteôÙms.

This was a veôÙry inôÙteôÙresôÙting in the senôÙse that moôÙre than this was acôÙtuaôÙllôÙy ôÙpreôÙsenôÙteôÙd. In parôÙtiôÙcuôÙlar the idea of how the goôÙvernôÙment wanôÙts to acôÙtuaôÙlly own ôÙtheir sysôÙteôÙms, and theôÙreôÙfoôÙre they are now choosing open sourôÙce soôÙftwaôÙre. It ôÙgoes beôÙyond than meôÙreôÙly using PyôÙthon and DjanôÙgo: they are alôÙso miôÙgraôÙting the woôÙrksôÙtaôÙtion maôÙchiôÙnes to open sourôÙce soôÙftwaôÙre (UôÙbunôÙtu), and trôÙying to earn moôÙre ôÙconôÙtracôÙtors that are starôÙtups raôÙther than huôÙge mulôÙtiôÙnaôÙtioôÙnals wiôÙth proôÙprieôÙtaôÙrôÙy ôÙsoôÙftwaôÙre. The idea is stiôÙll the saôÙme: they want open daôÙta, transôÙpaôÙrenôÙcôÙy, and toôÙ acôÙtuaôÙlly own their sysôÙteôÙms.

TheôÙse sort of iniôÙtiaôÙtiôÙves are gaiôÙning moôÙre and moôÙre tracôÙtion in the EuôÙroôÙpean UôÙnioôÙn, as many ciôÙties are starôÙting to shiôÙft toôÙwarôÙds an open goôÙvernôÙmenôÙt, wiôÙth oôÙpen daôÙta, and moôÙre transôÙpaôÙrenôÙcôÙy. I look foôÙrward to seeing moôÙre proôÙjecôÙts in ôÙPôÙyôÙthon wiôÙth open sourôÙce teôÙchôÙnoôÙloôÙgieôÙs, at their GiôÙtHub acôÙcount [2], in the ôÙforôÙthôÙcoôÙming monôÙths.

The seôÙcond taôÙlk was about ConôÙda, and shorôÙtly theôÙreaôÙfter a brief inôÙtroôÙducôÙtioôÙn, the rest of the preôÙsenôÙtaôÙtion waôÙs ôÙmosôÙtly a deôÙmo (liôÙve coôÙdinôÙg! :-), on whiôÙch we saw seôÙveôÙral examôÙples of insôÙtaôÙllinôÙg ôÙpaôÙckaôÙges, exôÙporôÙting the enôÙviôÙronôÙmenôÙt, creaôÙting a new one from a temôÙplaôÙte, etc. It waôÙs uôÙseôÙful to see boôÙth the strenôÙgths and shorôÙtcoôÙmings of the tool, what can anôÙd ôÙcanôÙnot be doôÙne wiôÙth it, and how it can be useôÙful for deôÙveôÙloôÙpers that haôÙve toôÙ ôÙdeal wiôÙth seôÙveôÙral sysôÙtem deôÙpenôÙdenôÙcieôÙs.

The third and last taôÙlk, was about PLOôÙNE, a CMS proôÙjecôÙt wôÙriôÙtten in PyôÙthoôÙn, and wiôÙth its 15-ôÙyeaôÙr-old teôÙnuôÙre, is the olôÙdest PyôÙthoôÙn ôÙproôÙject (thing that I did not knoôÙw). It was inôÙteôÙresôÙting to learn about its hiôÙgh-ôÙleôÙvel arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtuôÙre, comôÙpoôÙnenôÙts, and the way they woôÙrk. AboôÙve thaôÙt, I woulôÙd ôÙlike to hiôÙghôÙliôÙght the reôÙcap that it was doôÙne about many unôÙdeôÙraôÙppreôÙciated zoôÙpe liôÙbraôÙrieôÙs, that haôÙve been avaiôÙlaôÙble in PyôÙthon for a long tiôÙme, doôÙing an amaôÙzinôÙg ôÙjoôÙb. It is now in my lisôÙt, to reôÙview theôÙm.

All in all, it was a good meeôÙtuôÙp, and I liked veôÙry muôÙch the teôÙchôÙniôÙcaôÙl ôÙleôÙvel of the taôÙlks, and the fact that the toôÙpiôÙcs weôÙre veôÙry diôÙverôÙse, yeôÙt inôÙteôÙresôÙtinôÙg. I would like to conôÙtiôÙnue attenôÙding theôÙse meeôÙtupôÙs, and in the ôÙfuôÙtuôÙre I miôÙght even suôÙbmit a taôÙlk.

[1]https://www.meetup.com/es-ES/python-185/events/235058828/
[2]https://github.com/AjuntamentdeBarcelona

item.title: Oncoming events of Python and Architecture in September
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
El prôÙû°xiôÙmo sûÀôÙbaôÙdo 10 de SepôÙtiemôÙbre, esôÙtaôÙrûˋ preôÙsenôÙtanôÙdo mi charôÙla "Clean coôÙde in PyôÙthoôÙn", de ôÙla û¤lôÙtiôÙma EuôÙroôÙPôÙyôÙthon 2016, en un evenôÙto de PyôÙthon loôÙcal en Cû°rôÙdoôÙba, ArôÙgenôÙtiôÙna [1] [2].

La otra seôÙmaôÙna, esôÙtaôÙrûˋ asisôÙtienôÙdo a un meeôÙtup loôÙcal de arôÙquiôÙrecôÙtuôÙra (arôÙqôÙconôÙf), en donôÙde se preôÙsenôÙtaôÙrûÀ uôÙna charôÙla soôÙbre OpenSôÙtaôÙck [3] y su forôÙma de traôÙbaôÙjo disôÙtriôÙbuûÙôÙda.

EsôÙpeôÙro disôÙfruôÙtar de amôÙbos evenôÙtoôÙs!

[1]https://www.meetup.com/Cordoba-Python-Meetup/events/233741709/
[2]https://medium.com/@toniabdala/juntada-python-c%C3%B3rdoba-septiembre-db4abe5c4858#.yo0x1mqe3
[3]https://www.meetup.com/ArquitecturaDeSoftware/events/233824352/
